articles.title: Heatwave relief for Odisha: IMD predicts Kalbaisakhi rain and thunderstorms for 3 days - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
There is likely to be some relief from the prevailing heat conditions in Odisha, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) predicting a dip in temperature over the next three days.
The fall in temperature is expected due to Kalbaisakhi activity in several parts of the state, which is likely to bring rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds. In western Odisha, the mercury may drop by around 2 to 3 degrees Celsius, offering temporary respite from the scorching heat.
However, this relief is expected to be short-lived, as heatwave conditions along with humid weather are likely to return from May 3.
Kalbaisakhi activity across districts
On Tuesday, Kalbaisakhi was witnessed in several parts of the state. Areas like Mohana in Gajapati district, Digapahandi in Ganjam and parts of Boudh district experienced strong winds along with hailstorm and rain.

The sudden change in weather brought down the temperature in these areas, though it also caused localised disruptions. In Digapahandi, a coconut tree was reportedly burnt after being struck by lightning, highlighting the intensity of the storm activity.
